title: dactylorhiza majalis var. eboracensis
intro: Dactylorhiza majalis var. eboracensis, also known as the Yorkshire Orchid, is a sub-species of the summer-respiring Dactylorhiza majalis, a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza majalis var. eboracensis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as an infusion or tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
